name: Wisconsin (United States)

colors.symbolism: the deep-blue field traditional to U.S. state flags derived from regimental colors
the inscription and elements of the state coat of arms

description: A navy-blue field charged centrally with the Wisconsin state coat of arms, with the word 'WISCONSIN' arched above in white capitals and the date '1848' inscribed below.
history: Wisconsin's first state flag was adopted in 1863 for use by Civil War regiments and consisted of the state coat of arms on a blue field. It was readopted as the official flag in 1913. In 1979 the design was modified to add the state name 'WISCONSIN' above the seal and the date '1848' (the year of admission to the Union) below, to make it easier to distinguish from other state flags. The 1979 version is the current flag.

features.symbolism: the state seal flanked by a sailor and a yeoman, supporting a shield charged with symbols of Wisconsin's industries (a plough, pick and shovel, an arm and hammer, and an anchor) around a U.S. coat of arms inescutcheon, with the state motto 'Forward' on a banner above and a badger crest, all surrounded by a cornucopia and a pile of thirteen lead ingots.
the year '1848', when Wisconsin was admitted to the Union
